title: host.containers.internal only bound to one address
body:
### Issue Description

When a container uses a network with multiple subnets, only one of them will be reflected for `host.containers.internal` in the generated `/etc/hosts` inside the container.

In my case, with the following modified default network config in `/etc/containers/networks/podman.json`:
```json
{
     "name": "podman",
     "id": "23ebbf40f03bec53d03bf0a1b1aab65cdc72c772788063ac09e2add2c67bcb9d",
     "driver": "bridge",
     "network_interface": "podman0",
     "created": "2025-12-18T16:39:40.862456097+01:00",
     "subnets": [
          {
               "subnet": "10.88.0.0/16",
               "gateway": "10.88.0.1"
          },
          {
               "subnet": "fd00:acab:1337::/48",
               "gateway": "fd00:acab:1337::1"
          }
     ],
     "ipv6_enabled": true,
     "internal": false,
     "dns_enabled": true,
     "ipam_options": {
          "driver": "host-local"
     },
     "containers": {}
}
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. set up a podman network with multiple subnets (i.e. modify the default network)
2. start up a container bound to it (i.e. `podman run --rm -it docker.io/alpine:latest`)
3. check the contents of `/etc/hosts` inside the container


### Describe the results you received

The `/etc/hosts` inside an empty `docker.io/alpine:latest` container is:
```
127.0.0.1	localhost
::1	localhost
10.88.0.1	host.containers.internal host.docker.internal
10.88.0.7	b4d1449a5a4a unruffled_hoover
fd00:acab:1337::6	b4d1449a5a4a unruffled_hoover
```

### Describe the results you expected

The `/etc/hosts` being something like:
```
127.0.0.1	localhost
::1	localhost
10.88.0.1	host.containers.internal host.docker.internal
fd00:acab:1337::1	host.containers.internal host.docker.internal
10.88.0.7	b4d1449a5a4a unruffled_hoover
fd00:acab:1337::6	b4d1449a5a4a unruffled_hoover
```

  cpuUtilization:

comments.body: Do multiple IPv4 subnets work? Does it work if you set only an IPv6 subnet?

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).

> Do multiple IPv4 subnets work?

No.

```bash
sudo podman network create --subnet '10.89.1.0/24' --subnet '10.89.2.0/24' testnet
sudo podman run --rm -it --network=testnet 'docker.io/alpine:latest' cat /etc/hosts
```

results in the following `/etc/hosts` inside the container:
```hosts
127.0.0.1	localhost
::1	localhost
10.89.1.1	host.containers.internal host.docker.internal
10.89.1.4	d154359b7922 cranky_jackson
10.89.2.4	d154359b7922 cranky_jackson
```

<hr/>

> Does it work if you set only an IPv6 subnet?

Yes.

Curiously there doesn't seem to be a way to create an IPv6-only network through the Podman CLI directly as it just picks an IPv4 subnet to add to it anyway:
```bash
sudo podman network create --ipv6 --subnet 'fd64:d5a7:8b9b::/48' testnet6
```

Results in the following `/etc/containers/networks/testnet6.json`:
```json
{
     "name": "testnet6",
     "id": "00aa4354abe5396edc4a5527a3781765155dcbf28e27f574e8db4fdd68398827",
     "driver": "bridge",
     "network_interface": "podman2",
     "created": "2025-12-24T20:44:59.026094148+01:00",
     "subnets": [
          {
               "subnet": "fd64:d5a7:8b9b::/48",
               "gateway": "fd64:d5a7:8b9b::1"
          },
          {
               "subnet": "10.89.0.0/24",
               "gateway": "10.89.0.1"
          }
     ],
     "ipv6_enabled": true,
     "internal": false,
     "dns_enabled": true,
     "ipam_options": {
          "driver": "host-local"
     }
}
```

After removing that IPv4 subnet manually and running `sudo podman network update testnet6`:
```bash
sudo podman run --rm -it --network=testnet6 'docker.io/alpine:latest' cat /etc/hosts
```
returns
```hosts
127.0.0.1	localhost
::1	localhost
fd64:d5a7:8b9b::1	host.containers.internal host.docker.internal
fd64:d5a7:8b9b::2	0df059091007 vibrant_mayer
```

<hr/>

So it looks like Podman only assigns `host.containers.internal` to the gateway of the first subnet of the network.

Why do you want multiple addresses, in general host.containers.internal is best effort. If you need to connect to a specifc host ip it is best to be specific about that one and not rely on this alias. We only guarantee this it points to one host ip

The simplest reason is that having only one address is a problem when doing dual stack networking. If at least there was a preference for IPv6, it would allow IPv6-mapped IPv4 addresses.

Due to the puzzling [current preference for IPv4](https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/b0f86df5a66598b80ddf7d4cbd88165981d3370c/common/libnetwork/etchosts/ip.go#L88), this makes `host.containers.internal` just not usable for IPv6 when the container network is dual-stack.

I think the simplest solution is to include all the addresses and let the application in the container decide, this stops the IPv6 problems and would also not cause issues for the hypothetical application that only supports legacy IP despite IPv6 being around for decades now.

It could also be potentially useful for things like reverse-proxy allowlists, since there you definitely want all the addresses the host could talk to you from.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
So, to be clear, the ask here is to have `host.containers.internal` resolve to both an IPv4 and IPv6 address if dual-stack networking is available?

Essentially yes.

There's also the question of what should happen if there is more than one subnet per IP version, whether to include more than one address per IP version in that case.
